title: Aerospace Engineer
description: Aerospace engineers design, develop, test, and supervise the manufacturing of aircraft, spacecraft, satellites, and missiles. They work on everything from commercial airliners to defense systems and space exploration vehicles.
content: Aerospace engineers apply principles of engineering, science, and mathematics to create innovative solutions in the aerospace industry. In India, they contribute to ISRO's space programs, DRDO's defense projects, and the growing private aviation sector. This career demands strong analytical skills, creativity, and a passion for pushing the boundaries of flight and space travel.

financialFeasibility.note: Engineering education, especially from top institutions, can be expensive. While scholarships and educational loans are available, access is easier for students from middle-class and affluent backgrounds.

sectorScore.remarks: Significant opportunities exist in both government (ISRO, DRDO, HAL) and private sectors (Boeing India, Airbus Engineering, Tata Advanced Systems). The private sector is rapidly expanding in India.
